question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the `CutoverServiceImpl.get()` synchronized method block indefinitely (because it is waiting on a database call inside `getLastRecordFile()`), causing the `@Scheduled` thread to be held, and since `CutoverServiceImpl.get()` is `synchronized`, also blocking any concurrent `BlockFileSource` thread that tries to call `cutoverService.get()`, creating a cross-component deadlock?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that delivers a record file with version field set to exactly `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION` (the boundary value), causing the `recordFile.getVersion() < ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION` check to be false and `super.setStreamFileIndex()` to be skipped, but the protobuf `block_number` field is absent or zero due to a truncated network response, resulting in the record file being indexed at block 0?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `recordFile.getSidecars()` return a list where some `SidecarFile` objects have null `getTypes()` lists (due to partial protobuf deserialization over a partitioned connection), causing a NullPointerException in `sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains)` that terminates the Flux?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `Utility.archiveFile()` receive a valid file path but null bytes (because `sidecar.getBytes()` was set to null by a prior error path), causing a NullPointerException in the archive operation when `downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles()` is true, which propagates out of the Flux map and terminates sidecar processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `downloadNextBatch()` throw a `SignatureVerificationException` (because signature files from enough nodes are unreachable to reach consensus), which is caught and logged at WARN level in the parent, causing the downloader to silently skip the current batch and advance no progress, while the external user can sustain this condition to permanently halt ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords)` receive a `SidecarFile` with a null `getRecords()` list (because the sidecar was partially read), causing a NullPointerException inside the `flatMapIterable` operator that terminates the Flux and propagates out of `.block()` as an unchecked exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename)` succeed but return a `StreamFileData` with a `getFilePath()` that points to a non-existent or inaccessible path (because the local cache was not populated due to the partition), causing `Utility.archiveFile()` to throw a `FileOperationException` that terminates the sidecar Flux?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the `CompositeStreamFileProvider` mark all providers as unhealthy (via `markUnhealthy()` in `shouldRetry()`), causing `getProvider()` to return null, which then causes a NullPointerException in the `flatMap(p -> p.get(streamFilename))` call, propagating as an unhandled error through the download pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the `collect(Multimaps.toMultimap(...))` terminal operator receive duplicate `consensusTimestamp` keys from different sidecar files (because a retry over a partitioned network delivered the same sidecar twice), causing the `ArrayListMultimap` to accumulate duplicate `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries that are then associated with `RecordItem` objects, corrupting the sidecar record count?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `sidecarFileReader.read(sidecar, streamFileData)` throw an unchecked exception (e.g., due to a malformed protobuf from a truncated network response), which is not caught in `getSidecar()`, propagates through the Flux `map` operator, and terminates the entire sidecar pipeline, causing `onVerified()` to fail and the record file to not be committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `cutoverService.getLastRecordFile()` throw a `RuntimeException` (e.g., due to a database connection failure), which propagates out of the lambda passed to `cutoverService.get()`, and since `CutoverServiceImpl.get()` has a try-finally block, the exception propagates to `download()`, which has no try-catch, causing the `@Scheduled` executor to receive an unhandled exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `recordFile.getItems()` return a list of `RecordItem` objects whose `getTransactionRecord().getConsensusTimestamp()` values do not match any key in the `records` multimap (because the sidecar timestamps were corrupted in transit), causing all sidecar associations to be silently skipped and the record file to be committed with no sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename)` return a Mono that emits a `FileOperationException`, which is mapped to a `TransientProviderException` at the provider level, but since `getSidecar()` does not handle `TransientProviderException`, it propagates through the Flux and causes the entire sidecar download to abort without retry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the `@Scheduled` `download()` method accumulate multiple concurrent invocations (if the Spring scheduler does not enforce single-threaded execution for `fixedDelay`), causing multiple threads to simultaneously call `cutoverService.get()` and `downloadNextBatch()`, leading to race conditions on `lastStreamFile` and duplicate record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `Flux.fromIterable(recordFile.getSidecars())` receive a `ConcurrentModificationException` (if `recordFile.getSidecars()` is a live collection modified by another thread during the partition-induced retry), causing the Flux to terminate with an error that propagates out of `.block()`?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `sidecar.getHash()` return null (because the sidecar metadata was partially received), causing `Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash())` to return false (null != actual), throwing a `HashMismatchException` with a null expected hash that may cause a NullPointerException in `DomainUtils.bytesToHex(null)` inside the exception constructor?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the `streamFileProvider.list()` call in `downloadAndParseSigFiles()` time out for all nodes, causing `sigFilesMap` to be empty, and since the network is not `DEMO`, the downloader does not disable itself but instead retries on every tick, creating a sustained polling storm against the cloud storage provider?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `getSidecar()` return a `SidecarFile` with an empty `getRecords()` list (because the sidecar content was not fully received), causing `flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords)` to emit nothing, so the `records` multimap is empty, and all `RecordItem` objects in the record file are committed with no sidecar associations, silently losing sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes `importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(streamFileData)` return a path on a network-mounted filesystem that becomes unavailable during the partition, causing `Utility.archiveFile()` to throw an IOException that propagates as a `FileOperationException` through the Flux, terminating sidecar processing?
